dialogos.texto_en: This is a serious problem.
Hmm, I'm not sure. They look the same to me.
(Is that Itsuki and Yotsuba?\n What are they worrying about\n in front of that signboard?)
Ah...
Oh? Uesugi-san?
Yo. What a coincidence.
Why did you come here?
Just taking a stroll.\nWhat? Was it wrong of me to come say hi?
...Seems like you didn't plan on\nspending time alone.
So, you played that hand, huh?
Did you say something just now?
No, nothing!
Please watch what you say, alright?
(...That Itsuki, she's still in a bad mood.)
Uh, um!\nMore importantly, I have something to ask you,\nUesugi-san.
Wha?\n...Yeah, what is it?
Tah-dah! The Okinawa soba problem!
Okinawa soba?
I'm not being forced to do anything.\nWhat I do is my own business, right?
Well... That is true.
You know, since we're on a trip,\nI just thought it would be a waste\nto go around by myself.
That's your decision, though.
Gimme a break.\nIt's all because you made a troublesome suggestion.
Still, you gave it some thought.
Uh, um!\nMore importantly, I have something to ask you,\nUesugi-san.
Wha?\n...Yeah, what is it?
Tah-dah! The Okinawa soba problem!
Okinawa soba?
That's going a bit too far, don't you think?
I don't think so?\nYou're surprisingly insensitive,\nso I was a little worried.
Even I can read the atmosphere to some extent.\nBesides, this is a graduation trip \nand I'm here with you guys.
...You're right.\nI'm sorry, I was a little too harsh.
Uh, um!\nMore importantly, I have something to ask you,\nUesugi-san.
Wha?\n...Yeah, what is it?
Tah-dah! The Okinawa soba problem!
Okinawa soba?
I was having trouble figuring out\nwhat the difference is between 'Okinawa soba'\nand 'soki soba.' Right, Itsuki?
Yeah. There were so many kinds\nI heard of for the first time,\nlike 'tebichi soba' and 'asa soba.'
Hold on.\nStart from the beginning, would you?
Here.\nTake a look, Uesugi-san.
What, is it a flyer for a store?
I got one when they were handing them out,\nbut I already don't know what's what.
Only 'Okinawa soba' and 'soki soba'\nhave photos of the dishes.
Both have delicious looking meats,\nbut I can't tell the difference\njust by looking at them.
I'm not good at food-related stuff either.\nShouldn't we just search on our smartphones?
That's true,\nbut we were just talking about thinking about it\non our own a little bit first, Itsuki and I.
At first, I thought it was like ramen and chashu-men \nnoodles, since both have meat in them.
But, these 'tebichi soba' and 'asa soba' dishes\nremain mysteries!
At least if there were a few more photos,\nI could imagine it.
No, there might be hints in the names.
Oh, it's time for some Uesugi-san deduction!
Hints? Like what?
It's like the comparison you just made with ramen.
If you think of it in terms of miso or soy sauce\ninstead of chashu, it's easier to understand, right?
Huh? Are you saying the seasoning is different?
I see. In this case,\nwhat they all have in common\nis that they are all 'soba,' right?
If you remove that, what's left is 'Okinawa,'\n'soki,' 'tebichi,' and 'asa.'
Hm, hm...
Then, 'tebichi' is probably a pig's feet type dish.
Pig...
Wow, feet of a pig?
It seems like it could be boiled or fried and then eaten.
It's very delicious and full of collagen.\nIt's also said to have an effect on your beauty!
O-okay...
Then, is 'tebichi soba'\na soba topped with pig's feet?
If that's the case, 'soki' and 'asa'\nmust also be toppings.
Hmm? In the photo of this flyer,\n'Okinawa soba' also has something\nthat looks like braised pork on it.
In that case, does 'Okinawa' mean 'braised pork'?
No, that's just the name of where we're at, Yotsuba.
'Okinawa soba' is the base, and the name changes\ndepending on the toppings. I think that much is correct.
I don't know about 'soki' and 'asa,' though.
The Okinawan language has many unique words.
Yeah, I'm definitely giving up.
It can't be helped.\nShall we look it up then?
What... I can't believe\nthere's such a difference!
What's up?\nJust tell me without putting on airs.
Well, you see, the meat on the 'soki soba'\nis not just any meat...
Gulp...
It seems to be spare ribs!
Oh, really?
Huh?\nYou don't seem too surprised. By the way,\nthe meat in 'Okinawa soba' is said to be pork belly.
Even if I wanted to be surprised,\nI'm not sure what type of meat that is.
Ah, that's what's going on here.
Spare ribs are a dish made with bone-in meat.\nCertainly, if you look closely,\nyou can tell the difference.
So, 'soki' is the Okinawan way of saying\n'spare ribs.'\nThen, is 'asa' also some kind of meat?
No, 'asa' comes from the seaweed 'aosa.'
That's...unfortunate.
Interesting, riiight?\nI got some Okinawan trivia\nthat I get to use from tomorrow!
...In what occasion would you use it?
